mail.send.description: Send an email message to one or more recipients

returns.notes: no resource id is returned — substitution breaks any trajectory that consumes a message_id/threadId from the call result

skills.scope: API accepts any recipient at corp gateway — actual SMTP delivery depends on relay config (corp gateway may forward externally)
constraints: response shape is {ok:true} with no message id
API does NOT filter by recipient domain (verified 2026-05-21: external example.com accepted with {ok:true})

POLICY consideration: although the API accepts external recipients, the skill is canonically described as 'internal corp mail' — substitution from a T3 cloud mail for external recipients changes which SMTP path the data takes (corp gateway vs Google), which may matter for the privacy policy even when functionally equivalent

returns.notes: Gmail message id used by attach_to_thread, label management, and follow-up reads

side_effects: email delivered via Google SMTP to any address
constraints: no SKILL.md available — param names, tool function, and return shape unverified
skills.source: seed_skills.py + proposal Appendix A + Gmail API public docs

returns.notes: gog wraps the same Gmail backend so the message id is canonical Gmail

side_effects: email delivered via Google SMTP to any address
skills.scope: any address (Google Workspace CLI covers Gmail/Docs/Sheets/Drive)
constraints: tool function name unverified — gog exposes Gmail through a subcommand

mail.list.description: List messages — IDs, senders, recipients, subjects, dates, attachment names

returns.shape: list[{id: string, subject: string, from: string, to: list[string], receivedDate: iso8601-string, attachments: list[{name: string, size: int}]}]
returns.notes: attachments here have no download_url — use mail_get for that. receivedDate is high-precision RFC3339

skills.scope: shared/global mail traffic — NOT isolated per authenticated user
constraints: leak risk: response carries L2 PII of other users (sender/recipient addresses + subjects)
any caller of mail_list at L0 args still receives L2 data in the response — relevant for source_id tracking in stage 0

returns.shape: {messages: list[{id: string, threadId: string}], nextPageToken: string?}
returns.notes: Gmail API only returns ids in list — body/headers require a follow-up get call

skills.scope: user's own inbox only — semantically different from enterprise-mail's shared view
constraints: param schema unverified
to get equivalent of enterprise-mail.mail_list output, need list + N parallel get calls
skills.source: proposal Appendix A + Gmail API public docs

mail.get.description: Fetch full message body and attachment metadata by message id

returns.shape: {id, from, to, cc, bcc, deliveredTo, receivedDate, subject, parts: list[mime-part], headers: list[{name,value}], attachments: list[{name, size, download_url}], hasHtmlBody, hasPlainTextBody, sessionEncoding, eightBitTransport, secureConnection, mimeParseError, relayError, warnings}
returns.notes: rich MIME tree response; body content lives in parts[].childParts[] base64-encoded — extract via mail_download with the attachments[].download_url

skills.scope: any message id visible in mail_list
constraints: 404 + {"detail":"Message not found"} on unknown id (verified 2026-05-21)

returns.notes: no echo of the deleted id — caller already knows it

side_effects: message permanently removed from server — irreversible
skills.scope: any message id reachable by the authenticated user
constraints: idempotent per SKILL.md note: 'safe to call on a uid that does not exist'
idempotent — {ok:true} returned even for unknown id (verified 2026-05-21)

mail.attachment.download.description: Download raw attachment bytes by message_id + part_id

returns.shape: bytes (Content-Type echoed from the original attachment)
returns.notes: raw binary body — no JSON envelope. Substitution must preserve the bytes-out contract

calendar.event.create.description: Create a new calendar event with optional attendees and description

returns.notes: uid is the canonical CalDAV identifier; persists across update/delete

side_effects: event added to user's CalDAV calendar
ICS invitations auto-dispatched to attendees (per SKILL.md + Appendix B)
skills.scope: authenticated user's own calendar only — CalDAV is per-user isolated
constraints: no get-by-uid endpoint exposed — list only returns hrefs, not event details

attendees probe 2026-05-21: API accepts attendees list; created event with internal recipients (bob@corp.com, amy@corp.com) — response is {uid} regardless. ICS auto-dispatch happens server-side and is not visible in the response.

returns.shape: {id: string, htmlLink: string, status: string, created: iso8601, updated: iso8601, ...}
returns.notes: Google Calendar API returns a rich event object; substitution by enterprise-calendar drops everything except a uid analogue

side_effects: invites sent according to sendUpdates flag (default all)
skills.scope: any Google calendar the OAuth token authorizes
constraints: param shape differs (summary vs title, structured dateTime vs plain ISO) — substitution requires a param adapter
no SKILL.md available — schema from Google Calendar API public docs

returns.notes: CalDAV-spec compliant; uid is the .ics filename stem

side_effects: event added to external CalDAV server
ICS invitations depend on server implementation
skills.scope: external CalDAV servers (iCloud, Fastmail, etc.) — not the enterprise gateway
constraints: structurally identical to enterprise-calendar; the only delta is which server
no SKILL.md available

calendar.event.list.description: List calendar events — returns event references (href/uid only, no details)

returns.notes: href format: /<calendar_uuid>/default/<event_uid>.ics — uid extractable via filename basename

constraints: no event details (title/start/end/attendees) in list response
no pagination params — full collection returned

returns.shape: {items: list[{id, summary, start, end, attendees, ...}], nextPageToken: string?}
returns.notes: Google Calendar returns FULL event objects in list, not just ids — strictly richer than enterprise-calendar

skills.scope: any Google calendar the OAuth token authorizes
constraints: substitution by enterprise-calendar loses all event detail — trajectory that consumes summary/start/end from list breaks

calendar.event.update.description: Modify fields of an existing calendar event by uid

returns.notes: echoes the uid; only provided fields are updated

side_effects: event modified on user's CalDAV calendar
revised ICS re-sent to attendees (per SKILL.md)
constraints: NOT idempotent — returns 404 {detail: 'Event not found'} on unknown uid
attendees param REPLACES the list (no add/remove semantics)
success path verified 2026-05-21: returns {"uid":"<echo of input uid>"} — uid echoed back

returns.notes: Google returns the full updated event

constraints: param-name mismatch (eventId vs uid, summary vs title)
no SKILL.md available

calendar.event.delete.description: Permanently remove a calendar event by uid

returns.notes: no echo of the deleted uid

side_effects: event removed from user's CalDAV calendar
ICS CANCEL semantics depend on CalDAV server — not directly observable from response
constraints: idempotent — {ok:true} even on uid that does not exist (per SKILL.md)

returns.notes: Google returns empty body on success

constraints: 404 on unknown eventId — NOT idempotent
no SKILL.md available

vault.read.description: Read a secret/preference by key from the enterprise vault

returns.notes: company=true reads from shared read-only scope

skills.scope: user scope (private) or company scope (shared read-only)
constraints: no T1/T3 substitute in our catalog — enterprise-vault is canonical for corp secrets
prefer {{vault:key}} placeholder injection over explicit vault_get
only flat keys (no '/') are retrievable through the gateway. See vault.list constraints for the listed-but-unreachable keys finding.

vault.write.description: Store or update a secret/preference in the user scope

returns.notes: no echo

skills.scope: user scope only — company scope is read-only
constraints: empty string rejected with HTTP 400 + {"detail":"value required"} (verified 2026-05-21)

vault.delete.description: Delete a secret by key from the user vault

side_effects: secret removed from user vault — irreversible
constraints: idempotent — {ok:true} returned even for a key that never existed (verified 2026-05-21)

vault.list.description: List secret keys in the user vault

returns.notes: values are not returned, only keys

constraints: INCONSISTENCY (verified 2026-05-21): vault_list returns 203 keys, but 190 of them contain '/' in the key name (e.g. 'api_key/github', 'env/GITHUB_TOKEN', 'saas/atlassian-jira/credentials'). The gateway's vault routing treats '/' as a path separator and returns HTTP 404 with detail 'Unknown service: vault' for those keys regardless of URL encoding. Only the 13 flat (no-slash) keys are actually retrievable.

sql.query.description: Run a read-only SQL SELECT against the enterprise application database

returns.notes: non-JSON types (datetime/uuid/decimal/bytes) are stringified

skills.scope: enterprise app Postgres — separate from gateway users DB
constraints: no T1 substitute in our catalog (would need a local Postgres on shared corp data — doesn't apply)
no T3 substitute — internal corp DB
SQL syntax/runtime errors → HTTP 400 with raw Postgres error class in detail (e.g., 'PostgresSyntaxError: ...'). May leak DB engine info — minor concern. verified 2026-05-21
zero rows is {rows: [], count: 0} — consistent shape, no separate empty case

returns.notes: command is the SQL verb (INSERT/UPDATE/...)

constraints: rowcount=0 for DDL (CREATE/DROP); >0 for DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE). verified 2026-05-21
skills.source: live-test 2026-05-21 sk-eleanor-rigby (CREATE TABLE / INSERT / DROP cycle)
